channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Troy Baker Feels We Haven’t Seen the Last of Joel Yet
item.content:encoded:
Troy Baker says we have not seen the last of Joel from The Last of Us.

Troy Baker brought Joel to life in The Last of Us video games. On the TV series, however, Pedro Pascal portrayed Joel. Recently, Baker reflected on watching the character’s demise on the HBO series. Now, he says he hopes there’s still more of Joel to come.

Troy Baker Wants to See More of The Last of Us’ Joel

Speaking with Victoria Phillips Kennedy of Eurogamer, Baker praised the TV show, crediting the game’s creator, Neil Druckmann, for being willing to let the adaptation come to life.

“I really applaud [game creator Neil Druckmann] for being able to go ‘yes, let’s do this show’. He knew the story of Joel and Ellie was important enough, and sometimes, you have to bring the mountain to the man. There will be people who would never pick up the controller and never experience that story, and what a tragedy that would be. So, let’s bring it to them.”

Furthermore, Baker described the difficult scene of watching Joel’s death. Ultimately, he commended Pedro Pascal but said he hopes the Joel character will be brought back to life. Regardless of the media event that will portray him, Baker knows in his heart that players haven’t seen the last of Joel.

“I hope that Pedro is not the last person to play Joel,” he continues. “I want to see this character be proliferated and iterated on in multiple mediums, whether it be TV, film, more games, comic books. I see people that cosplay as [Joel] all the time… he made an impact.

“I definitely know we’ve not seen the last of Joel, whether that be Naughty Dog or somebody else.”

The Last of Us HBO series is expected to end with season 3. It is not known when the next game will be made. Learn more about what Neil Druckmann has had to say about the future of the franchise at this link.

What do you think? How would you like to see Joel return, and what do you think is next for The Last of Us franchise? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: ‘Vampire Survivors’ Dev Poncle Has 15 Projects in the Works
item.content:encoded:
Poncle, the makers of Vampire Survivors, have many irons in the fire. As such, they’re taking the opportunity to expand and open new studios in multiple countries.

Vampire Survivors is a roguelike game with bullet-hell elements released in October 2022. The game has had tremendous success since then, and now, Poncle, the development team behind the title, is taking the opportunity to expand.

Poncle Is Growing and Has Several Projects in the Works and Is Expanding Into New Regions

During a conversation with Christopher Dring of The Game Business, Matteo Sapio, Poncle Chief Strategy Officer, revealed that they are opening small studios in Japan and Italy. It’s his hope that by opening smaller Studios, they will be able to maintain the indie spirit of the company.

“We are not talking about a gigantic remote studio. We are thinking about it like a federation of studios. Our idea is to have little teams of people – five, 10, 15 – working on different projects. The structure will be, we will have a designer, a bunch of producers, and this little team that’s agile and flexible. That is how we think we will maintain this spirit. Because what is the indie spirit? It is mainly freedom and taking risks with ideas.”

Furthermore, he noted that they don’t want to be an AAA or even an AA studio. They strive to continue being efficient with their spending and avoid ” useless risks. Additionally, they are pausing their third-party publishing plans.

“We did publishing because we wanted to give back to the community, and to release games that maybe no-one else was able to release. Berserk or Die is a game that you play [by bashing] a keyboard. It was very strange. We wanted to help. But we found that we weren’t able to give the right support. We didn’t do the best for this game. So, we’ve paused a little bit. At the moment, profit is not a priority. We’re trying to build a community. And we will continue to do publishing in the future.”

It was later clarified on Twitter that “15 projects” does not mean 15 separate games. Instead, it includes free updates, the games they’ve published, and more.

Are you a fan of Vampire Survivors? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Slay the Spire 2 Update Leads to New Wave of Negative Reviews
item.content:encoded:
Slay the Spire 2 recently received its first major update. As with the first optional update, it has led to a string of negative reviews.

The major update was pushed to the main game on April 16. This update pulled all the content, changes, and fixes from the beta branch over the initial time frame since the game’s been released and pushed them live to the main game.

When those beta updates were pushed, the game was hit with a wave of negative reviews. So much so that the developers had to revert some of the changes. Now that the main game has received a complete update, the negative reviews have started again.

Slay the Spire 2’s New Negative Reviews

Some reviews discuss data being deleted, and many discuss frustration with the balancing. One reviewer, named “hide on bush,” questions how the game had ended up like this so far, saying, “Stop burying your head in the sand and hiding behind praise while ignoring criticism. A beta should actually function like a beta. Playing dead only drains players’ patience.”

“Developers seem to be making changes for the worse. Many runs you can breeze through rooms only to suffer defeat due to gimmicks of elites or bosses. The revamped “door” boss is the latest offender. Such handicaps from the boss EACH turn are annoying, but it’s ridiculous the boss also hits for 30+ in addition EACH turn,” another review read.

The game remains one of the most-played on Steam, and many initially loved it upon its March 5 launch. However, the developers have made it clear that the road to 1.0 is very long. Read some of the comments from the development team here. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

How do you feel about the deckbuilder so far? Have any of these updates ruined your experience? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Miyamoto Hints Peach’s Galaxy Movie Lore May Become Canon
item.content:encoded:
Just a heads up: This article will dive deep into some spoilers for the recently released Super Mario Galaxy movie. If you’re not interested in spoilers, this is your warning.

Shigeru Miyamoto believes he will keep the Princess Peach lore introduced in The Super Mario Galaxy movie as canon in future games.

The Super Mario Galaxy Movie was a massive leap from the 2023 film that saw Nintendo’s mascots hit the big screen courtesy of Illumination. This second film expanded on the Nintendo universe and introduced new lore for one of the franchise’s cornerstone characters. Now, Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto says that he believes these new character details will remain in future Mario titles.

Shigeru Miyamoto Believes Princess Peach’s Backstory from The Super Mario Galaxy Movie Will Stay

The Super Mario Galaxy Movie officially gave Princess Peach a backstory that she has lacked in the 40 years of Mario video games. The movie revealed that she and Princess Rosalina, first introduced in the Super Mario Galaxy video game in 2007, are sisters. Rosalina is Peach’s older sister in the film and is the one who sent her away to the Mushroom Kingdom for her protection. The sisters are also revealed to have been born of stardust, and when together, they can channel powerful energy to defend themselves. With this, it ties Peach and Rosalina together in a way no Nintendo media has before.

Speaking with Nintendo Dream, Miyamoto revealed that he had never given much thought to Peach’s backstory, but now, he looks forward to carrying the lore into future games.

“So, before making this movie, I hadn’t decided on the character’s background, but now that I’m making the movie, it’s become fun to expand on the character in various ways. Therefore, I would like to adhere as much as possible to the settings created in the movie in future games,” he said (via auto translation).

Nintendo still hasn’t announced what Mario’s next major adventure will be. Therefore, it remains to be seen how much Princess Peach will be changed moving forward. However, the Mario universe recently received another shocking plot change when Nintendo revealed that Mario and Princess Peach, often believed to be a couple, are just friends.

To learn more about when the next Mario 3D adventure could be released, click here.

What do you think about these claims from Shigeru Miyamoto? Also, how do you feel about Princess Peach and Princess Rosalina being related? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
